name: phase_c_per_query_pack_budget_experiment
host_notes: Phase C experiment record — null result. The plan-real-corpus-eval.md follow-up identified per-query pack budget as 'the single biggest remaining chart-improvement available' for lifting multi-required-entity skills (multi_hop_lineage on dbt, multi_pr_series + adr_landing on github) off 0%. This file records the empirical test that disproves that hypothesis. Code change tested then reverted; no scenario-code diff in the commit that lands this file.
hypothesis: Bumping the pack-builder max_items budget per-query to scale with len(required_coverage) will lift multi-required-entity skills from 0% by giving the right entities room to fit alongside high-rank distractors.

dbt_corpus_convergence.interpretation: multi_hop_lineage budget bumped 8 -> 12, coverage_mean did move up to 0.333 (from prior 0.0) so the pack now contains 2 of 6 required entities on average — but threshold is 0.6 (4/6), so success_rate stays 0%. Larger packs feed the noise loop more candidates, modestly hurting useful_delta.

github_corpus_convergence.interpretation: No skill flipped. multi_pr_series Q2 budget bumped 8 -> 10, but coverage_mean stays 0.0 — the formula doesn't help when the seed extractor can't anchor the query (no PR numbers in 'Phase 1 through Phase 4 PRs that shipped scenarios 5.1, 5.2, 5.3'). adr_landing is unaffected because its required_coverage is 3 entities and max(8, 6) = 8.

conclusion: Hypothesis disproved. Pack budget is not the bottleneck for the 0%-success skills on either corpus. Two distinct upstream causes account for the remaining failures: (a) seed extraction can't anchor intents that name no entity directly (multi_pr_series, layer_classification, author_attribution); (b) coverage threshold of 0.6 against required_coverage sizes >= 5 means even with full GraphSearch hits, displacement by adjacent neighbors keeps coverage_mean below 0.6 (multi_hop_lineage at 0.33 coverage_mean is the clearest example).
what_to_try_next_instead: Phrase-based seed extraction — extract 'Phase 1 through Phase 4', 'ADR for terminology', 'authored by dependabot' as filter-able predicates rather than entity-name lookups. Highest-leverage; lifts multi_pr_series, layer_classification, author_attribution all at once.

Investigate adr_landing 1.0 -> 0.0 regression under GraphSearch (separate from this experiment, surfaced in phase_b2_2026-05-07_github_trellis_baseline_no_graphsearch.json). Likely fitness-loop interaction or graph-traversal over-fetching, not pack budget.
GraphSearch ranking that prefers seed-direct nodes over 1-hop neighbors when the budget binds. Should help adr_landing without per-query budget tuning.
decision_recorded: Per-query pack budget reverted from both scenarios (no committed code change). The experiment record stays committed because the negative result is the value here — it points the next iteration at seed extraction rather than at budget tuning.
